security.title: Use of Singleton Pattern Without Synchronization in a Multithreaded Context
security.description: The product uses the singleton pattern when creating a resource within a multithreaded environment.

evidence.explanation: WindowClassRegistrar::GetInstance() (lines 63-68) implements unsynchronized double-checked locking without any lock/mutex protection. Multiple threads can simultaneously pass the if (!instance_) check and create multiple instances of the singleton.

security.title: Missing Synchronization
security.description: The product utilizes a shared resource in a concurrent manner but does not attempt to synchronize access to the resource.

evidence.explanation: Static int g_active_window_count (line 30) is incremented in Win32Window constructor (line 114) and decremented in destructor (line 118) without any synchronization. Race conditions can occur if multiple threads create/destroy Win32Window objects concurrently.
